courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean I

meetings.location: Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G02
Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G02
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 804

courses.description: This course invites students without prior knowledge of Korean to explore the basic features of the Korean language. Supported by audiovisual aids, students will receive intensive practice in spoken and written Korean through interactive class work, homework and online exercises. They will become familiar with the basic concepts of Korean grammar and learn how to interact in some simple everyday situations.
courses.enrollment_requirement: Not for students who have taken KORE2000,2001,2050(1010),3000,3001,3002,3050 or 3051
courses.learning_outcomes: At the end of the course, students will be able to speak, read and write simple Han Geul (Korean writing system) having familiarity with current Korean society and its culture. They will be able to speak about topics such as everyday greetings, asking and giving information, shopping.
courses.course_syllabus:
| Course Content: The course will cover Unit 1 – 5 of the textbook “Active Korean 1” | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lesson | Speech acts | Grammar |
| **Unit 1** **Hangeul** | **Introduction to ‘Han geul’** * About Korea * History of the language * Consonants and Vowels | - vowels and consonants - Diphthongs / Aspirated and forced consonants - Some pronunciation rules - Syllable structure - Sentence structure |
| **Unit 2** **Greetings & Introduction** | **Greetings & Introductions** * Introducing people (jobs, nationalities) * Asking the names of thing | -P- Basic sentence structure - Subject marker -이/가 -Topic/Contrast marker -은/는 -Polite ‘Verb-‘be’ ending -예요/이에요& its negation: -이/가 아니에요 |
| **Unit3** **Restaurant** | **Expressions used at restaurants** * Counting things (Native Korean numbers) * Ordering at a restaurant | -V(으)세요 (To use honorific suffix) -N 주세요 (“Give me N, please”) -Measure Nouns (-개/명/장/잔/권…) |
| **Unit4** **Shopping** | **Expressions related to shopping** * Buying things in a store * Talking about the location | -N이/가 A(Subject marker used with A) N이/가 있어요/ 없어요 -이거/저거/그거 -Numbers (Sino-Number) - Marker also, too: -도 - Object marker -을/를 |
| **Unit 5** **Daily life** | **Questions related to daily life** * Talking about schedule * One’s likes and dislikes * Telling time * Telling days of the week | -A/V-아/어/여요(Informal Verb-ending) -N 을/를 (Object Marker) -N 에 가다/오다(go /come to somewhere) -안 A/V (Negation of A/V) -못 V : can not V -V고 (and)/ V지만 (but) Conjunctions: 그리고, 그러나, 그런데 그렇지만, 그래서, 하지만, 왜냐하면 |
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our language programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. Students are very welcome to provide comments and feedback on the course any time to their course teacher or the course level coordinator through email or in personal conversation. In addition, students’ feedbacks will be collected in the middle of the term through an open-end questionnaire and the teacher will discuss the feedbacks in class and make improvements if necessary. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.
courses.required_readings:
Required textbook:
\* Active Korean 1 2006. Language Education Institute Seoul National University
The book is available at Swindon University Bookstore.

\* Sogang New Korean 1A 2008. Korean Language Education Center, Sogang University, Korea.
(Used as Supplementary, Students are not required to purchase)

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean II

meetings.location: Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G01
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 408
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 408
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 408
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 408
William M W Mong Eng Bldg 402

courses.description: Building on the knowledge acquired in KORE1000, this course aims to further develop students’ ability to communicate in Korean in various everyday situations. It also seeks to give students a taste of Korean culture and invites them to explore cultural differences. Through intensive practice students will develop more confidence in interacting in Korean while further expanding and solidifying their understanding of the basic grammar and pronunciation patterns.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Not for students who have taken KORE2001,2050(1010),3000,3001,3002,3050(2010) or 3051(3010)
Prerequisite: KORE1000 (1001)
courses.learning_outcomes: The student should be able to master the basic communication skills such as talking about daily activities and habit, relating past events, asking for direction and location, using public transportation and making a phone call.
courses.course_syllabus:
| Course Content: The course will cover Unit 6 – 9 of the textbook “Active Korean 1” | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lesson | Speech acts | Grammar |
| **Unit 6** **Time** | * Talking about daily life * Expressing negation * Expressing days of the week * Telling the time when something happens | - Describing a person or a thing - Linking two sentences S-지만 S’, S-고 S’ - Time : \_시 \_분 - N에,에서 - 안 A/V and 못A/V |
| **Unit 7** **Appointment** | * Making suggestions * Giving date * Talking about something you did in the past * Making appointment | -을/ㄹ까요? - Past tense -까지 -시간, 분 -월, 요일 |
| **Unit 8** **Locations and Directions** | * Giving the location of an item * Asking for and giving directions * Asking how long it takes to the destination * Asking how to take subway line | -N이/가 있어요/ 없어요 -위/아래/앞/뒤/옆/안 -(으)로 가다/오다 -까지 -에서 |
| **Unit 9** **Phone call** | * Telephone conversation * Expressing an action in progress * Expressing the politeness * Expressing the future and the supposition | -v**-**이지요? (Ask to confirm the statement) - v-아/어/여 주다 (polite offer) - v-고 있다 - ㄹ/을 거예요 - Honorific suffix: -(으)시 - Adjectives |

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean III
courses.description: This course aims to further enhance students’ communication skills in the four language domains listening, speaking, reading and writing. Students are expected to communicate confidently in a wider range of social interactions including more formal situations and simple professional contexts as well as reporting past events and sharing experience. At the end of this course, students will have completed a proficiency level comparable to level 1 of the Test of Proficiency in Korean (TOPIK).
courses.enrollment_requirement:
1.Not for students who have taken KORE3000,3001,3002,3050(2010) or 3051(3010)
2.Prerequisite: KORE2000 (1002) or 2050 (1010)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon successful completion of the course, students should be able to
(1)improve their basic communication skills with expanded vocabulary, expressions and grammar.
(2)know how to address strangers or staff with questions and requests using the polite formal verb endings.
(3)be more accustomed to Korean traditional and modern culture and social life.
courses.course_syllabus:
| Course Content: The course will cover unit 1 – 5 of the textbook “Active Korean 2” | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lesson | Speech acts | Grammar |
| **Unit 1** **Family** | **Expressions related to introducing family members :** * talking about relations of family members and one’s seniors/elders * Using personal pronouns | N의N’ (possessive form) N(이)세요 (identification of the subject) 누구 (who) A/V-(으)세요/-(으)셨어요 (honorific identification of the subject) 무슨N (what kind of N) |
| **Unit 2** **Transportation** | **Expressions when using transportation:** * Explaining how to get home using public transportation * Asking and answering how to get to somewhere/where to get off and how long it takes * Talking about what one should do | N에서N’을/를 타다 (get on a transportation in somewhere) N에서 내리다 (get off at a place) N에서N’(으)로 갈아타다 (transfer into another transportation at a place) N을/를 타고 가다/오다 (go/come by a transportation) ‘ㄷ’ irregular verbs |
| **Unit 3** **Reason** | **Expressions needed when giving excuses:** * Talking about what one cannot or could not do * Making an appointment * Giving a reason/two or more alternatives | 못V ( cannot do V) A/V-아/어/여서 (Because……..) ‘ㅂ’ irregular adjectives N (이)라서 (Because N is N’) N(이)나 N’ (either N or N’) |
| **Unit 4** **Post Office** | **Expressions needed at the post office:** * Sending a parcel * Talking about conditions * Telling what’s inside * Telling what to do in each situation | V-ㄹ/을 거예요(future tense/to make a supposition) N(으)로 (by means of N) A/V-(으)면 (if…A/V…) N한테 (to a person ( recipient)) |
| **Unit 5** **Reservations** | **Expressions when making reservations:** * Talking about possibility or ability * Buying tickets * Describing types of room when making reservations * Discussing length of time | V-ㄹ/을 수 있다 (can/ be able to V ) A-ㄴ/은데요/V-고 싶다 (expressions for wanting) N 동안 (during N) N부터 (from N : when defining a time or distance) |

The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean IV
courses.description: This course aims at increasing students’ communicative fluency in oral and written Korean. Using a variety of classroom activities and multimedia, students will build up their competence in the four language domains listening, speaking, reading and writing. Through intensive practice they will be able to communicate in a wide range of social situations with the appropriate awareness of the pragmatic rules involved. Students receive guidance in the process of composing longer texts displaying a range of registers and styles relating to past, present and future events.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
1.Not for students who have taken KORE3001,3002,3050(2010) or 3051(3010)
2.Prerequisite: KORE2001
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon successful completion of the course, students should be able to
(1)improve the overall communication skills with enlarged linguistic knowledge
(2)Along with the oral practice, students will improve writing skill adequately with written verb-ending form
(3)be more accustomed to Korean culture and society
courses.course_syllabus:
| Course Content: The course will cover unit 6 – 9 of the textbook “Active Korean 2” | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lesson | Speech acts | Grammar |
| **Unit 6** **Family** | **Expressions related to etiquette** **Expressions related to permission and agreement** * Expressing permissions * Expressing prohibitions * -Expressing a point of time | * V-아/어/여도 된다 * V-(으)면 안 되다 * A/V –(으)ㄹ 때 |
| **Unit 7** **Transportation** | **Expressions used in a hospital** * Expressing where it hurts * Explaining why one came to see the doctor * Describing symptoms * Giving a reason * Expressing prohibitions | * Irregular verbs & adjectives * N이/가 아프다 * N도 * A/V-(으)니까 * V-지 마세요 |
| **Unit 8** **Reason** | **Expressions of agreement** **Expressions related to worries** * Asking for and giving advice * Making suggestions | * N은/는 N에/한테 좋다/나쁘다 * V-아/어/여 보세요 * V-는 게 어때요? * ‘ㄹ’irregular verbs & adjectives |
| **Unit 9** **Post Office** | **Expressions related to shopping** * Buying something at a store * Recommending something * Exchanging * Comparing | * Noun Modifying Adjective: A-ㄴ/은N * Sentence V-고 S’ * N을/를 N’로 바꾸다 * N보다 (더) , 제일A/V * ㄴ/은 거 |
| **Supplementary Expressions** | * V-ㄹ/을 줄 알다/모르다: know/don’t know how to V * V-(으)려고 하다 : be going to V * V-아/어/여 보다: to express a trial or past experience * N (이)나/ V거나 : either.. or * V-아/어/여도: although V | |
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our language programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean V
courses.description: This course focuses on the further development of communication skills in Korean with emphasis on the expression of personal views and the training of argumentative strategies. The course will also engage students in critical reflections on various aspects of life in Korean and their own social environment. Students will be guided to analyze various types of data to support their views. They will learn how to express their wishes and aspirations using the appropriate registers.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
1.Not for students who have taken KORE3002 or 3051 (3010) 
2.Pre-requisite: KORE3000 (2002) or 3050 (2010)
courses.course_syllabus:
| **Unit Topics** | **Expressions** | **Vocabulary** | **Grammar** | **Reading Text** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Unit 1** **Experience** **경험** | -For discussing experience | * Famous places * Counting words for days * Food | * -아/어/여 봤다: one’s attempt at doing something * -vㄴ/은 적(이) 있/없다:have (not) experienced something * -았/었/였을 때: the point of time that an incident happened * N 전/후에 before/after N | * Stone tower * A few Reading texts on different topics will be uploaded in Moodle |
| **Unit 2** **Hobbies** **취미** | -For discussing hobbies -of preference -talking about what one can do | * Related to hobbies | * -는 거: being attached to a verb stem to change the verb to a noun form. * -‘르’ irregular verbs and adjectives * -(으)ㄹ 줄 알다/모르다: know how to V * 잘 V, 잘 못V | * Taekwondo * A few Reading texts on different topics will be uploaded in Moodle |
| **Unit 3** **Employment** **취업** | -For interviews | * Related to majors * Related to work | * S-ㄴ/은/는데 S’ * -기 때문에 * -겠 :for speaker’s will or intention | * Why won’t they eat miyeokguk (seaweed soup) * A few Reading texts on different topics will be uploaded in Moodle |
| **Unit 4** **Fashion** **유행** | -Used at beauty salons -For talking about what’s in fashion | * Related to beauty salon * Related to fashion | * -는N : Nound modifying present action Verb * -게 = -것이 * -(으)러 가다/오다 * N처럼 * 어떤 N * -ㄹ/을 게요: The first person’s verb ending to express speaker’s will/intention | * Dongdaemun Market * A few Reading texts on different topics will be uploaded in Moodle |
| **Unit 5** **Out of order** **고장** | -Related to breakdowns and repairs | * Related to breakdowns and repairs * Machines | * **반말** **Verb-Ending** + (이)야: + ㄹ/을게 + ㄹ/을 거야 * -ㄴ/은/을 N | * Let’s do away with formality * A few Reading texts on different topics will be uploaded in Moodle |

The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean VI
courses.description: This course builds on the knowledge acquired through previous Korean language courses. It aims at leading students to a proficiency level comparable to the threshold from level 2 to level 3 of the Test of Proficiency in Korean (TOPIK). To achieve this goal, students will be encouraged to work project-based through independent or collaborative work with their peers. At the end of this course students will have mastered the most important grammar features of Korean and be able to communicate effectively in a wide range of everyday situations and a number of professional contexts. They will be able to use their oral and written language skills to relate to, reflect on and analyze past and current issues involving the Korean society.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Not for students who have taken KORE3051 (3010). 
Prerequisite: KORE3001.
courses.course_syllabus: This course builds on the knowledge acquired through previous Korean language courses. It aims at leading students to a proficiency level comparable to the threshold from level 2 to level 3 of the Test of Proficiency in Korean (TOPIK). To achieve this goal, students will be encouraged to work project-based through independent or collaborative work with their peers. At the end of this course students will have mastered the most important grammar features of Korean and be able to communicate effectively in a wide range of everyday situations and a number of professional contexts. They will be able to use their oral and written language skills to relate to, reflect on and analyze past and current issues involving the Korean society.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. Students are very welcome to provide comments and feedback on the course any time to their course teacher or the course level coordinator through email or in personal conversation. In addition, students’ feedbacks will be collected in the middle of the term through an open-end questionnaire and the teacher will discuss the feedbacks in class and make improvements if necessary. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.
courses.required_readings:
• Seoul University Korean Language Center. ‘Active Korean’. Course book with Audio CD & its Work book

• Sogang University Korean Language Center. ‘Sogang Korean’. Course book with Audio CD & its Work book

• Ewha University Korean Language Center. ‘Ewha Korean’. Course book with Audio CD & its Work book

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Sounds of Korean
courses.description: This course introduces the phonetic system of the Korean language. Supported by audiovisual aids, students will understand the sound of the vowels and consonants, the pronunciation rules and the intonations in different contexts of the Korean language. The articulation of some Korean sounds that are more difficult for Chinese speakers will also be examined. Through interactive classroom learning activities, students’ accuracy of Korean pronunciation will be enhanced.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Prerequisites: KORE3000,3001,3002,3050 or 3051
Not for students who have taken KORE3100 in 2nd term 2018-19 and 1st term 2019-20
courses.course_syllabus:
| **Topic** | **Content** |
| --- | --- |
| ***Vowels*** ***and*** ***Consonants*** | ***Vowels*** 1. Single-vowels: ㅏ, ㅓ, ㅗ, ㅜ, ㅡ, ㅣ, ㅔ, ㅐ 2. Double-vowels: ㅘ, ㅝ, ㅟ, ㅚ, ㅞ, ㅙ / ㅑ, ㅕ, ㅛ, ㅠ, ㅖ, ㅒ, ㅢ |
| ***Consonants*** 1. Sounds of basic consonants: ㅁ, ㅂ, ㄴ, ㄷ, ㄹ, ㅅ, ㅈ, ㄱ, ㅇ, ㅎ 2. Aspiration: ㅋ, ㅌ, ㅍ, ㅊ 3. Glottal tension: ㄲ, ㄸ, ㅃ, ㅆ, ㅉ |
| ***Syllable-final consonants*** 1. Single-final consonants: [ㄱ], [ㄴ], [ㄷ], [ㄹ], [ㅁ], [ㅂ], [ㅇ] 2. Double-final consonants |
| ***Pronunciation Rules*** | * ***Linking***: 한국어[한구거], 앉아요[안자요], 좋아요[조아요] etc. * ***Glottalization***: 학교[학꾜], 앉다[안따], 할 수[쑤] 있다, 여권[여꿘], etc. * ***Nasalization***: 박물관[방물관], 음료수[음뇨수], etc. * ***Aspiration***: 어떻게[어떠케], etc. * ***Lateralization***: 설날[설랄], etc. * ***Insertion of ‘******ㄴ******’***: 무슨 일[무슨닐], etc. |
| ***Intonation*** | 1. Intonation of statements 2. Intonation of questions 3. Intonation of propositive sentences 4. Intonation of imperative sentences 5. Intonation of exclamational sentences |
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our language programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. Students are very welcome to provide comments and feedback on the course any time to their course teacher or the course level coordinator through email or in personal conversation. In addition, students’ feedbacks will be collected in the middle of the term through an open-end questionnaire and the teacher will discuss the feedbacks in class and make improvements if necessary. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.
courses.required_readings: There is no particular textbook for this course. Handouts will be given by the teacher.

외국인을 위한 한국어 발음 47 -1, Language Education Institute, Seoul National University, Korea Language Plus, 2009.
외국인을 위한 한국어 발음 47 -2, Language Education Institute, Seoul National University, Korea Language Plus, 2009.
서울대 한국어 1A – Student’s book, Language Education Institute, Seoul National University, Two Ponds, 2013.
서울대 한국어 1B – Student’s book, Language Education Institute, Seoul National University, Two Ponds, 2013.
서울대 한국어 2A – Student’s book, Language Education Institute, Seoul National University, Two Ponds, 2013.

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean through Folktales
courses.description: This course is a combination of content learning and language learning. Making use of famous Korean folktales, students will enrich their understanding of the Korean language as well as their knowledge about Korean folktales and culture. The tales taught will be read in simplified versions in Korean.
courses.course_syllabus:
| Unit | Topic | Grammar | Expression |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. Pasque flower 할미꽃 | Filial duty 효도 | V-곤 하다, V-아/어/여 버리다, A/V-(으)ㄴ/는 척하다 | Onomatopoeia/mimetic words related to crying 의성어 의태어 |
| 2. The good brothers 의좋은 형제 | Close bond among siblings형제 간의 우애 | A/V-(으)ㄴ/는 법이다, V-고 말았다, A/V-(으)ㄹ 리가 있다/없다 | Onomatopoeia/mimetic words related to laughter |
| 3. Magic pot 요술항아리 | Greed 욕심 | A/V-(으)ㄹ 수밖에 없다 (으)ㄹ지도 모르다 V-다가는 | Onomatopoeia/mimetic words related to complaint |
| 4. the longest name in the world 세상에서 가장 긴 이름 | Meaning of names 이름의 의미 | V/A-(으)ㄹ 정도로 V-는 한 V-(으)ㄹ 뻔하다 | Four Character idioms 사자성어 |
| 5. Faithful Magpies 은혜갚은 까치 | Repay in kind 은혜 | V-느라고 V-고도, N-조차, V/A-(으)ㄹ 텐데 | Onomatopoeia/mimetic words related to sleep 관용어, 속담, 사자성어 |
| 6. The nose of a pig 돼지의 코 | 한국에서의 동물들 이미지 Images of animals in Korea | V-도록 V/A-기만 하다 V/A-은/는 커녕 | Onomatopoeia/mimetic words related to animal sound 관용어, 속담, 사자성어 |
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our language programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. Students are very welcome to provide comments and feedback on the course any time to their course teacher or the course level coordinator through email or in personal conversation. In addition, students’ feedbacks will be collected in the middle of the term through an open-end questionnaire and the teacher will discuss the feedbacks in class and make improvements if necessary. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedbacks will be used for future course planning and teaching.
courses.required_readings:
이야기로 배우는 한국어 2015 (주) 박이정
Customized class material will be provided as well.
courses.recommended_readings:
Web Resources:
- Korean language learning
<http://www.sejonghakdang.org/>
- Vocabulary & Grammar
<http://www.korean.go.kr>
<http://www.koreanwikiproject.com/>

Readings & Library Resources:
Korean Grammar in Use, Min Jin-Young (Darakwon,2010)
Learn Korean thru Idioms and Proverbs, 관용어와 속담으로 배우는 한국어, 원은영, 이경아 (도서출판 참, 2017)
Korean mimetic words and onomatopoeia, 한국어 의성어 의태어 (Dongyang books, 2014)
Korean proverbs and four-word Chinese phrase,한국어 속담, 한자성어 (Dongyang books, 2015)
Everyday Korean Idiomatic Expressions, TalkToMeInKorean (Longtail Books, 2015)

courses.subject: KORE
courses.title: Korean through Media
courses.description: This course is designed to help students improve daily conversational skills learned from Korean media such as dramas, news, advertisements, and K-pops. With exposure to various communicative situations through Korean media, students will be able to learn colloquial Korean and different styles of speech while understanding Korean culture and society deeply. This class will provide an opportunity to share students’ learning of media expressions, phrases, and grammar, through classroom activities, assignments, and group projects using their creativity. Students will also be encouraged to take their learning further, scrutinize differences in expressions of Korean media, and discover similarities and differences between Korean media expressions and those from their own countries. All the materials and the medium of instruction will be in Korean.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Upon successful completion of the course, students will be able to:
- identify native Korean colloquial expressions used in media and apply them in real-life contexts
- speak, listen to, read, and write using expressions, vocabulary, and sentences from media effectively and recognize the proper usage
- expand the knowledge of Korean culture, society, and customs
- share learned knowledge confidently with other classmates/peers and develop self-learning techniques
- connect expressions from each media with grammar and culture and internalize them in the long run
courses.course_syllabus:
| **Course Content** | | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Topic** | **Sources** | **Expression** | **Grammar** | **Culture** |
| Sport | -Sport News -Drama <Twenty-Five Twenty-One> | -Typical terms and expressions used in news covering athletic competitions | -거든 if (something is true), then~ -듯이 as if -아/어 대다 to keep on doing | Korean street cheering culture (Red Devils) |
| Four seasons & Weather | -Weather forecast -Drama <People of the Meteorological Agency> -K Pop <Cherry Blossom Ending> | -Lyrical expressions that symbolize the four seasons -Weather-related proverbs and idioms | -에 따라서according to -겠 will, would -A(으)ㄴ/V는 탓에due to -ㄴ/는다고 하더라도 even if | Seasonal Festivals |
| K-Wave | K-Entertainment Report | -Expressions for an interview with celebrities -Broadcasting related expressions | 간접화법 indirect speech | K- Wave’s impact on the global fan base |
| Contemporary issues of society and culture in Drama | 별에서 온 그대 < My Love from the Star> | -Mimetic words -Slangs and popular expressions in drama -Words with multiple meanings | -든지-든지 either - or - -더니 and then -았/었더니 as a result | Korean Celebrity culture |
| 사내 맞선 <Office Matchmaker> | -Idioms & slangs | -는 바람에 due to -척하다 pretend to -(으)ㄹ 테니까since | Blind-dating culture |
| 며느라기 <Mackerel Run> | -Dialects -Talking about wish | -(으)ㄴ 가 보다/나 보다 seems like -(으)랴 –(으)랴 trying to -(으)ㅁ으로써 through | Low Birthrate problem |
| 미생 < Incomplete Life> | -Business related expressions -Idioms | -겠습니다 shall -(으)ㄹ 뻔하다 nearly, almost -(으)ㄹ까 봐서be worried/afraid that -다시피 하다 almost like | Unemployment problems (Permanent worker/temporary worker) |
| Hope, Comfort, and Love for the Youth through K-Pop | 밤 편지 IU <Through the Night> | -Lyrical expressions and metaphors in the lyrics of the songs | -(으)ㄹ게요 will, shall -기 바라다 hope, wish ~ 것 같다 appears to be -아/어지다 피동사 become, get | Korea’s contemporary social image and culture in the lyrics of the song |
| 모든 날 모든 순간 Paul Kim <Every Day, Every Moment> | -(으)ㄹ 만큼 as much as -게 하다 사동사causative verb -처럼 like, as |
| 수고했어 오늘도 Rooftop Moonlight <Good job, Today Too> | -는다고 하더라도 although -아/어 두다 do something in advance -았/었던 had been |
| 별보러 가자 Jeok Jae <Let’s Go See the Stars> | -아무도, 아무것도, 아무데도 nobody, nothing, nowhere -더라 it turns out -(으)ㄹ래 would like to |
| Teamwork & Competition | 런닝맨 Entertainment program <Running Man> | - Intimate speech style - Idioms - Neologism | -noun+되다 passive -ㄴ/는다기보다 rather than -길래 because | Traditional Korean game |
| Economy & Consumer Lifestyle | 홈쇼핑 광고 Advertisement | -Typical colloquial expressions used in advertisement -Expressions for consumer lifestyle | -피동사 passive verb | Fast delivery speed (Rocket delivery) |
courses.feedback_evaluation: Our language programme highly values students’ feedback and comments and is happy to use them for reflection on our teaching and improvement. Students are very welcome to provide comments and feedback on the course at any time to their course teacher or the course level coordinator through email or in personal conversation. In addition, students’ feedback will be collected in the middle of the term through an open-ended questionnaire and the teacher will discuss the feedback in class and make improvements if necessary. The course will also follow the university’s course evaluation exercise at the end of the term, and students’ feedback will be used for future course planning and teaching.
courses.required_readings: No textbook is required; videos and notes will be provided in class.
